messages.content: Place the following events in the correct chronological order 1 Louis XVI askes nobles to help him reform the tax code 2 Tennis Court Oath 3 Meeting of Estates General 4 King Louis XVI takes the throne

4) King Louis XVI takes the throne
1) Louis XVI asks nobles to help him reform the tax code
3) Meeting of the Estates-General
2) Tennis Court Oath

King Louis XVI ascended to the throne of France in 1774. As financial difficulties in the country continued to escalate, largely due to costly wars and royal extravagance, he was forced to attempt a reform of the tax system to stave off bankruptcy. He initially called upon the nobles and other privileged classes to contribute more, but they refused. To resolve this impasse, Louis XVI convoked the Estates-General in 1789, which had not been called since 1614. During the Estates-General, the Third Estate, representing the common people, found themselves locked out of the meetings and consequently took the Tennis Court Oath on June 20, 1789, swearing not to disband until a new constitution was established.

After the Battle of Waterloo, Napoleon was exiled to the Island of Elba


After the Battle of Waterloo, Napoleon was exiled to the Island of Elba


True

False

False.

Napoleon was exiled to the Island of Elba after his abdication following the Treaty of Fontainebleau in 1814, not after the Battle of Waterloo. After his escape from Elba and subsequent defeat at the Battle of Waterloo in 1815, Napoleon was exiled to the remote Island of Saint Helena in the South Atlantic Ocean where he spent the remainder of his life.

Who passed the Law of Prairial and the Law of Suspects?


Who passed the Law of Prairial and the Law of Suspects?


National Assembly


Napoleon Bonaparte


Committie of Public Safety


The Directory'

The Committee of Public Safety passed both the Law of Prairial and the Law of Suspects during the period of the French Revolution known as the Reign of Terror.

The Law of Suspects was passed on September 17, 1793, and allowed for the arrest of individuals who were deemed to be enemies of the Revolution or who were not fully supportive of it. The Law of Prairial, enacted on June 10, 1794, further accelerated the process by which individuals could be tried and executed without extensive evidence or rights of defense, thus intensifying the Reign of Terror.

he guillotine was the preferred method of execution during the American Revolution.


The guillotine was the preferred method of execution during the American Revolution.


True

False

False.

The guillotine was not used during the American Revolution; it was associated with the French Revolution, which began in 1789. The guillotine became notorious as a method of execution during the Reign of Terror in France. In the colonies that would become the United States, methods of execution during the time of the American Revolution typically included hanging, firing squad, or in some cases, other methods used by various states, but not the guillotine.

Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of Marie Antoinette?


Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of Marie Antoinette?


Rumors of overspending


Brother was the Prussian Emperor


She is the wife of Louis XIV


She was known to dominate her husband

She is the wife of Louis XIV

Marie Antoinette was not the wife of Louis XIV. She was the wife of Louis XVI. Louis XIV, also known as Louis the Great or the Sun King, reigned as King of France from 1643 until his death in 1715. Marie Antoinette, originally from Austria, married Louis XVI, and she became the Queen of France from 1774 until her husband's deposition in 1792. Her brother was the Holy Roman Emperor, Joseph II, not the Prussian Emperor. The Prussian Emperors belonged to a later period, and the title did not exist during Marie Antoinette's lifetime.

What Law was passed by the National Assembly on August 4, 1789?


What Law was passed by the National Assembly on August 4, 1789?


Declaration of the Rights of Man 


Estates General


Declaration of Independence


National Constitution of Man

On August 4, 1789, the National Assembly of France passed a decree abolishing feudal privileges. This event is known as the August Decrees. The decrees led to the dismantling of the feudal system that had long governed property and peasant obligations in France, effectively putting an end to many of the traditional rights of the nobility and the clergy.

The "Declaration of the Rights of Man and of the Citizen" (not to be confused with the August Decrees) was adopted later, on August 26, 1789. This document laid out the fundamental rights of all men (and, by some interpretations, women) and the principles of popular sovereignty, among other things.

The "Estates General" was not a law but the name of the representative assembly that preceded the National Assembly.

The "Declaration of Independence" is associated with the American Revolution and was adopted on July 4, 1776, not by the French National Assembly.

There is no such document as the "National Constitution of Man" in the context of the French Revolution. However, the French Constitution of 1791, which was the first written constitution of France, was completed and ratified by the National Assembly in September 1791, establishing a constitutional monarchy.
sow hast he answerr

The answer to the question "What Law was passed by the National Assembly on August 4, 1789?" is that the National Assembly passed a sweeping decree that abolished the feudal system in France, known as the August Decrees. These decrees effectively ended the traditional rights of the nobility and the clergy, such as personal serfdom and various dues and tithes that peasants were obligated to pay.

Here's a summarization for clarity:

On August 4, 1789, the National Assembly passed the August Decrees, abolishing feudal privileges and the feudal system in France, not a specific named "Law."

Post the Reign of Terror the Directory was formed by the constitution.   The Directory was made up of six directors.


Post the Reign of Terror the Directory was formed by the constitution.   The Directory was made up of six directors.


True

False

False.

The Directory, established by the Constitution of the Year III (1795) after the Reign of Terror, was made up of five directors, not six. It was the executive body of the French government from November 2, 1795, until November 9, 1799 (18 Brumaire VIII, according to the French Revolutionary calendar), when it was overthrown by Napoleon Bonaparte in the coup of 18 Brumaire.

Louis XVI swore an oath of loyalty to the new constitution in September of 1791.


Louis XVI swore an oath of loyalty to the new constitution in September of 1791.


True

False

True.

King Louis XVI did indeed swear an oath of loyalty to the new constitution, which was ratified by the National Constituent Assembly on September 3, 1791, establishing a constitutional monarchy in France. The Constitution of 1791 marked a significant shift away from absolute monarchy and attempted to balance power between the king and the newly formed Legislative Assembly. Louis XVI's oath to uphold this constitution occurred on September 14, 1791.

On December 2, 1804 Napoleon crowned himself Emperor


On December 2, 1804 Napoleon crowned himself Emperor


True

False

True.

On December 2, 1804, Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of the French in a lavish ceremony held at Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris. The act of crowning himself was a powerful symbol meant to show that he owed his position to no one but himself, as opposed to divine right or the blessing of the Church. Although Pope Pius VII was present at the ceremony, Napoleon famously took the crown from the Pope's hands and placed it on his own head. Later, he also crowned his wife, Josephine, as Empress.

Here are the descriptions correctly placed with each Estate:

1st Estate (Clergy):
- 1% of population
- Did not pay taxes
- No required military service

2nd Estate (Nobles/Aristocrats):
- 2% of population
- No required military service
- Did not pay taxes

3rd Estate:
- 97% of population
- Paid taxes to lord, king, and church
- Required military service

King Louis XVI inherited a lot of debt from his predecessors.


King Louis XVI inherited a lot of debt from his predecessors.


True

False

True.

King Louis XVI inherited a significant amount of debt when he ascended to the French throne. The financial situation of France was dire, exacerbated by years of costly wars, including funding the American Revolution, and extravagant spending by the royal court. Fiscal mismanagement and a lack of a coherent tax system, where the privileged classes (nobles and clergy) were exempt from many taxes, further strained the finances of the state. These economic problems played a significant role in precipitating the financial crises that led to the French Revolution.

What event led Louis XVI to move from Versailles to Paris?


What event led Louis XVI to move from Versailles to Paris?


Storming of the Bastille


Tennis Court Oath


Reign of Terror


Women’s March on Versailles

The event that led Louis XVI to move from Versailles to Paris was the Women's March on Versailles.

On October 5, 1789, an angry crowd of women, along with some male revolutionaries, marched from Paris to Versailles in response to the scarcity and high price of bread. The demonstrators forced the royal family to return with them to Paris. The next day, October 6, King Louis XVI, Queen Marie Antoinette, their children, and the royal entourage moved from the Palace of Versailles to the Tuileries Palace in Paris under the "protection" of the revolutionaries. This event effectively made the royal family captives of the Parisian populace and marked the symbolic decline of the king's power during the French Revolution.

By 1787, the French government was bankrupt for all of the following reasons EXCEPT


By 1787, the French government was bankrupt for all of the following reasons EXCEPT


corrupt tax system


wars


investing in the church of England


royals spent too much on luxuries

investing in the church of England

The French government's bankruptcy by 1787 was not due to investing in the Church of England. This was never a contributor to the French financial crisis. The other factors listed – a corrupt tax system, costly wars (including the American Revolutionary War), and the extravagant spending of the royal family on luxuries – all played significant roles in the financial collapse that precipitated the French Revolution.

King Louis XIV came to the throne at the age of 19 in the year 1774.


King Louis XIV came to the throne at the age of 19 in the year 1774.


True

False

False.

It was King Louis XVI, not King Louis XIV, who came to the throne at the age of 19 in the year 1774. King Louis XIV, known as the Sun King, reigned from 1643 until his death in 1715 and is famous for his long and absolute rule, as well as for building the Palace of Versailles. Louis XVI, on the other hand, was the last King of France before the fall of the monarchy during the French Revolution.

The Estates General was the legislative body of France during the reign of Louis XVI.  It had not been called to meet since 1614.


The Estates General was the legislative body of France during the reign of Louis XVI.  It had not been called to meet since 1614.


True

False

True.

The Estates General was indeed a legislative assembly of the different classes (or "estates") of French subjects. It had a separate assembly for each of the three estates (the clergy, the nobility, and the commoners), but it was not a regularly convening body. It had not been convened since 1614 until King Louis XVI called for a meeting in 1789 to address France's severe financial crisis. This meeting was one of the events that sparked the French Revolution.
